steps: Go to https://dash.cloudflare.com and log in or create a free account
Click your user icon in the top right, then select 'My Profile'
Click 'API Tokens' in the left sidebar
Click 'Create Token', then select 'Create Custom Token'
Under Permissions, add: Account > R2 Storage > Edit
Under Account Resources, select your account
Copy the token — it is shown only once
Note your Account ID from the Cloudflare dashboard URL or the right sidebar of the main dashboard page

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: API Token
credential_fields.description: Your Cloudflare API Token with R2 Storage permissions

notes: R2 bucket management uses Account-level permissions, not Zone-level
You will need your Account ID (visible in the Cloudflare dashboard URL: dash.cloudflare.com/<account_id>/r2)
R2 offers 10 GB free storage and 1 million free Class A operations per month
For S3-compatible object operations (upload/download), use R2 API tokens generated in the R2 section of the dashboard, not the API Token portal
The Cloudflare API token (this guide) is for bucket management only, not object operations
Token is shown only once — store it securely immediately after creation

test_endpoint.description: List R2 buckets to verify credentials (replace {account_id} with your account ID)
